📩 Zaujalo? Ozvěte se nebo pošlete profil, řekneme si bližší info k pozici.

title: Associate Linux Support Engineer
description_html:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1100+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder led, profitable and growing.

We are hiring an Associate Support Engineer to build a career with Canonical and support the technical needs of customers leveraging Ubuntu and open source products. This role is an opportunity for a hands-on engineer with eagerness to learn, a passion for Linux and open source technologies to work with some of the best people in the industry at Canonical.

We regularly solve interesting, technical problems. From triaging complex cloud environments, desktop and server issues, Linux kernel crashes we've never seen before to OS configuration issues and root cause analysis, we provide an expert level of support and satisfaction to our peer groups and customers. You will be accountable for delivering an outstanding technical support experience on the full technical stack at Canonical, across cloud, desktop and IoT. From problem qualification to full resolution, you will own and manage your cases. When needed, you will transfer your cases to your colleagues or involve senior engineers. This is a globally remote role.

Location: This is a globally remote role.

The role entails

  • Working from your remote home office to provide technical support for employees.
  • Being available to take ownership of new cases via telephone, email and web
  • Acting as an internal customer advocate keeping them updated in a timely manner
  • Ensuring that each support case owned by you is handled according to our Service Level Agreement (SLA) with the highest possible customer satisfaction by setting the right expectations and fulfilling your commitments.
  • Contributing new knowledge base articles and maintaining existing articles, ensuring shared knowledge and lessons learned are available to customers.
  • Drafting and distributing technical notices for internal and external communication.
  • Prioritising your work in order to accomplish the most important and urgent tasks first
  • Keeping on learning as our products and services grow and evolve
  • Understanding Ubuntu development process to be able to set customer expectations correctly on timeline for a fix
  • Maintaining regular communication and information exchange with the rest of the team, locally and remotely
  • Identifying and suggesting any opportunities to provide a better service
  • Participating in a regular weekend working rotation

What we are looking for in you

  • Exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
  • Undergraduate degree in a technical subject or a compelling narrative about your alternative chosen path. Preferable majors: Computer Science, MIS, Electrical Engineering, etc.
  • Confidence to respectfully speak up, exchange feedback, and share ideas without hesitation
  • Track record of going above-and-beyond expectations to achieve outstanding results
  • Experience with Linux troubleshooting (collecting logs and stack traces, fine-tuning by editing configuration files)
  • Knowledge or basic hands-on experience on 2 or more of:
    • Linux server administration
    • Programming fundamentals in any language (bash, Perl, Ruby, Javascript, C or C++, Go)
    • Networking (bonding, firewalling, bridging, switching, network file system tuning, MTU issues, etc.)
    • Virtualization / Cloud computing concepts (provisioning, monitoring, orchestration, etc.)
  • Ability to learn quickly, thrive on change and handle the pressure of a customer facing job
  • Strong written and verbal English communication skills
  • Appreciative of diversity, polite and effective in a multi-cultural, multi-national organisation
  • Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
  • Ability to travel internationally twice a year, for company events up to two weeks long
Noting that we require and will perform a background check for this role.

Nice-to-have skills

  • Experience supporting virtualized environments (KVM, Virtual Box, VMWare)
  • Experience with other open-source desktop technologies
  • Active contribution to open-source projects/forum/code

What we offer colleagues

We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.

  • Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
  • Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
  • Annual compensation review
  • Recognition rewards
  • Annual holiday leave
  • Maternity and paternity leave
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
  • Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Data Collector/ Data Annotator
description_html:
Why This Job Is Exciting


At Innovatrics, we equip you with the tools, context, and trust to thrive. You will have the unique opportunity to work on both sides of AI development: building the data foundations and actively trying to break our models to make them unhackable.


You’ll be a key player in improving models for Presentation Attack Detection (Liveness). This isn't just about following instructions; it’s about thinking like an attacker to find vulnerabilities in our remote identity verification systems.


In your first months, you’ll explore:


● Gaining proficiency in internal data collection/annotation applications and tools.

● Understanding the specific data requirements and quality standards for AI model training, especially in the context of remote identity verification and presentation attack detection.

● Learning the art of "Spoofing": Understanding how users might try to trick biometric systems (using photos, masks, or digital screens).

● Assisting with foundational data sorting and collection tasks using various devices and operating systems.

● Contributing to our goals, by annotating the available data.


As you settle into the role, you’ll start contributing by:


● Proactive Spoofing & Red Teaming: Brainstorming and executing creative ideas to "fool" our liveness models.

● Experimental Testing: Trying new approaches based on your own intuition or investigating specific issues reported by customers.

● Data Creation: Creating small, high-impact datasets based on your successful spoofing attempts to provide a baseline for our engineers to fix vulnerabilities.

● Taking ownership of the data collected by you, annotation (image/data sorting), and organization of diverse datasets according to project specifications.

● Collaborating regularly with the AI/ML teams to ensure the integrity and high quality of prepared data.

● Troubleshooting technical issues with data collection applications


Within your first year, you’ll have the opportunity to:


● Become a subject matter expert in Presentation Attack Detection, influencing how we defend against fraud.

● Potentially develop and implement basic scripts (e.g., in Python) to optimize data tasks and automate spoofing reports.

● Shape the collection process by identifying which types of "attacks" the models are currently most vulnerable to.


What You’ll Help Build


At Innovatrics, we build biometric technologies for elections, border control, digital ID, and secure onboarding in 80+ countries. Our algorithms have reached over a billion people.


Your work here won’t get lost in a backlog; it’ll shape real-world systems that matter.


We’re a small team, so your ideas ship fast. From day one, you’ll build production features and shape global-scale projects within weeks.


Join us if you're passionate about making a change in the results of critical, real-world AI applications in identity verification and biometrics.


Working Hours & Location


🌎 In-Office, Bratislava, Slovakia. This role requires presence in our Bratislava office to ensure secure data handling, efficient collaboration, and access to the necessary data collection devices and equipment.


Required locations: Bratislava, Slovakia.


About You

You are a detail-oriented and tech-savvy individual with exceptional organizational skills who takes pride in maintaining high levels of accuracy. You align strongly with our company values and mission, and you thrive in an environment that values precision, technical aptitude, and collaborative problem-solving.


Qualifications


● Creative Problem Solving: The ability to think outside the box to bypass security measures (spoofing).

● Strong attention to detail and a proven ability to maintain accuracy while managing large and complex datasets.

● Tech Versatility: Proficiency in utilizing a variety of devices (smartphones, tablets, cameras) and operating systems (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows) for efficient data collection and annotation tasks. ● Excellent organizational and time management skills with a demonstrated ability to troubleshoot technical issues in data collection tools.

● Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team to ensure collaborative data integrity.

● Preferred: Basic knowledge of Python

● Preferred: Familiarity with machine learning concepts and image annotation tools.


Compensation


At Innovatrics, we offer highly competitive salaries because we value top talent who share our values and goals. Compensation is determined based on your level, skills, experience, qualifications, and location.

title: Data Collector/ Data Annotator
description_html:
Why This Job Is Exciting

At Innovatrics, we equip you with the tools, context, and trust to thrive. You will have the unique opportunity to work on both sides of AI development: building the data foundations and actively trying to break our models to make them unhackable.

You'll be a key player in improving models for Presentation Attack Detection (Liveness). This isn't just about following instructions; it's about thinking like an attacker to find vulnerabilities in our remote identity verification systems.

In your first months, you'll explore...

  • Gaining proficiency in internal data collection/annotation applications and tools
  • Understanding the specific data requirements and quality standards for AI model training, especially in the context of remote identity verification and presentation attack detection
  • Learning the art of "Spoofing": Understanding how users might try to trick biometric systems (using photos, masks, or digital screens)
  • Assisting with foundational data sorting and collection tasks using various devices and operating systems
  • Contributing to our goals, by annotating the available data

As you settle into the role, you'll start contributing by...

  • Proactive Spoofing & Red Teaming: Brainstorming and executing creative ideas to "fool" our liveness models
  • Experimental Testing: Trying new approaches based on your own intuition or investigating specific issues reported by customers
  • Data Creation: Creating small, high-impact datasets based on your successful spoofing attempts to provide a baseline for our engineers to fix vulnerabilities
  • Taking ownership of the data collected by you, annotation (image/data sorting), and organization of diverse datasets according to project specifications
  • Collaborating regularly with the AI/ML teams to ensure the integrity and high quality of prepared data
  • Troubleshooting technical issues with data collection applications

Within your first year, you'll have the opportunity to...

  • Become a subject matter expert in Presentation Attack Detection, influencing how we defend against fraud
  • Potentially develop and implement basic scripts (e.g., in Python) to optimize data tasks and automate spoofing reports
  • Shape the collection process by identifying which types of "attacks" the models are currently most vulnerable to

What You'll Help Build

At Innovatrics, we build biometric technologies for elections, border control, digital ID, and secure onboarding in 80+ countries. Our algorithms have reached over a billion people.

Your work here won't get lost in a backlog; it'll shape real-world systems that matter.

We're a small team, so your ideas ship fast. From day one, you'll build production features and shape global-scale projects within weeks.

Join us if you're passionate about making a change in the results of critical, real-world AI applications in identity verification and biometrics.

Working Hours & Location

🌎 In-Office, Bratislava, Slovakia. This role requires presence in our Bratislava office to ensure secure data handling, efficient collaboration, and access to the necessary data collection devices and equipment. Required locations: Bratislava, Slovakia.

About You

You are a detail-oriented and tech-savvy individual with exceptional organizational skills who takes pride in maintaining high levels of accuracy. You align strongly with our company values and mission, and you thrive in an environment that values precision, technical aptitude, and collaborative problem-solving.

Qualifications

  • Creative Problem Solving: The ability to think outside the box to bypass security measures (spoofing)
  • Strong attention to detail and a proven ability to maintain accuracy while managing large and complex datasets
  • Tech Versatility: Proficiency in utilizing a variety of devices (smartphones, tablets, cameras) and operating systems (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows) for efficient data collection and annotation tasks
  • Excellent organizational and time management skills with a demonstrated ability to troubleshoot technical issues in data collection tools
  • Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team to ensure collaborative data integrity
  • Preferred: Basic knowledge of Python
  • Preferred: Familiarity with machine learning concepts and image annotation tools

Compensation

At Innovatrics, we offer highly competitive salaries because we value top talent who share our values and goals. Compensation is determined based on your level, skills, experience, qualifications, and location.

💶 Salary gross range: €2168 - 2711€, tied to competency and technical evaluation.

title: Cloud Engineer
description_html:
About The Role

The Azure Application Hosting team at Swiss Re is seeking a Cloud Engineer with a focus on Kubernetes and Azure Cloud. In this role, you'll engage in Infrastructure as Code, automation, and application hosting using Kubernetes. Your main responsibility will be to manage and improve our shared application hosting platform for various types of applications in the Azure cloud.

A significant part of your role involves the further engineering of the platform, but also ensuring the stability and operability of the platform. This includes automating operational processes as much as possible. You will also participate in an on-call rotation, approximately once every two months, to handle high-priority incidents, with the frequency expected to decrease as our team grows.

Your work will include managing a managed PaaS platform, covering aspects like fully automated secret management, storage, and identity management for application teams. You'll also be involved in migrating new applications to the public cloud and optimizing existing setups.

For Example, You Will

Build infrastructure and a platform that allow Swiss Re units to:

  • help improve the life of farmers in Brazil with image recognition based on satellite data.
  • build models to predict life expectancy or predict the CO2 footprint of companies.
  • support many other exciting projects in a collaborative environment with colleagues from Swiss Re's international offices.

You will work in a team building new cutting-edge solutions cloud infrastructure solutions, with 5 mentors to support your learning.

We are a diverse team and our passion to learn is what connects us. We are looking for a person who loves to learn and we will support you in this journey! We reserve time for studying and attending conferences, and we organize our own upskilling sessions as well. In addition to projects for our clients, we also work on innovation projects which allow us to learn new methodologies and gain fresh experience.

The team is based in Zürich, Switzerland, and Madrid, Spain. We're looking for someone who is ready to contribute to our team's efforts and support Swiss Re's cloud initiatives.

About You

Nobody is perfect and meets 100% of requirements. If you however meet some of the criteria below and are genuinely curious about the world of public cloud and application hosting, we will be happy to meet you.

To be successful in the role, you need to have these technical skills and knowledge:

  • Experience in Kubernetes and generally application hosting
  • Experience in public cloud, mainly Azure
  • Knowledge of interacting with API and CLI interfaces
  • Knowledge of programming language Golang and Bash/Powershell
  • Passion for learning and innovating

If you also have the following experience and interests, it is a huge plus:

  • Background in software engineering or application design
  • Knowledge of and/or interest in pulumi for IaC
  • Knowledge of and/or interest in platform and application security
  • Experience in corporate/enterprise technical landscapes

If you join us, you will be able to participate in a variety of interesting initiatives.

For Slovakia the base salary range for this position is between EUR 2,700 and EUR 4,500 per month (for a full-time role). The specific salary offered considers: -the requirements, scope, complexity and responsibilities of the role, -the applicant’s own profile including education/qualifications, expertise, specialisation, skills and experience. In addition to your base salary, Swiss Re offers an attractive performance-based variable compensation component, designed to recognise your achievements. Further you will enjoy a variety of global and location specific benefits. Eligibility may vary depending on the terms of Swiss Re policies and your employment contract.

About Swiss Re Corporate Solutions

Swiss Re is one of the world’s leading providers of reinsurance, insurance and other forms of insurance-based risk transfer. We anticipate and manage risks, from natural catastrophes and climate change to cybercrime.

Swiss Re Corporate Solutions is the commercial insurance arm of the Swiss Re Group. We offer innovative insurance solutions to large and midsized multinational corporations from our approximately 50 locations worldwide. We help clients mitigate their risk exposure, whilst our industry-leading claims service provides them with additional peace of mind.

Our success depends on our ability to build an inclusive culture encouraging fresh perspectives and innovative thinking. Swiss Re Corporate Solutions embraces a workplace where everyone has equal opportunities to thrive and develop professionally regardless of their age, gender, race, ethnicity, gender identity and/or expression, sexual orientation, physical or mental ability, skillset, thought or other characteristics. In our inclusive and flexible environment everyone can bring their authentic selves to work and their passion for sustainability.

If you are an experienced professional returning to the workforce after a career break, we encourage you to apply for open positions that match your skills and experience.

title: Data Engineer
description_html:
Our opportunity

Data Analyst (Catastrophe Modelling)

Support delivery of data-centric IT solutions for catastrophe portfolio modeling. Design and manage cloud-based data pipelines (Databricks/Azure), ensuring scalability, performance, and traceability.

Your role

As a Data Analyst, your main responsibilities will include:

  • Design, document, and oversee data ingestion and transformation pipelines on cloud platforms.
  • Translate business goals into clear requirements, user stories, and technical solutions.
  • Collaborate with risk modelers and technical teams on workflows for exposure data and catastrophe model results.
  • Analyze and transform diverse data sources for informed decision-making and regulatory compliance.
  • Facilitate agile ceremonies and ensure alignment between business and technical stakeholders.
  • Support development, testing, and release planning—emphasizing performance, security, and business value.
  • Advocate for robust data architecture; liaise with Data Architects and Information Security.
  • Provide functional consulting and drive adoption among internal users and data providers.

Your Skills And Experience

As a Data Analyst, your skills and experience will ideally include:

  • Expert T-SQL skills for complex querying and performance tuning.
  • Experience with data science tools (e.g., R, Python).
  • Strong background in data modeling, transformation, visualization, and governance.
  • Hands-on with data pipelines/ETL tools (Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Jenkins).
  • Able to design and manage data flows in cloud environments, familiarity with full software lifecycle.
  • Experience supporting legacy platforms and leading requirements workshops.
  • Skilled in stakeholder management within a matrixed organization.
  • Proven agile delivery (Scrum/Kanban).
  • Fluent in English.
  • Experience with UAT, release planning, and performance/security validation.
  • Familiarity with insurance risk modeling or exposure data.

Nice-to-Have:

  • Experience with Apache Spark, Delta Lake, Databricks.
  • Exposure to catastrophe modeling platforms (RiskLink, Touchstone).
  • Knowledge of regulatory reporting and internal risk frameworks.
  • Insurance and catastrophe modeling expertise.

We offer

In accordance with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, the minimum gross monthly compensation for this position, determined based on objective and gender-neutral criteria, is 2 900 EUR/gross. The final compensation package will depend on the candidate’s qualifications and relevant experience. 

Working Time Benefits

Personal days off, Concentrated work week, Additional vacation, Home office, Extra days off at occasion of childbirth, Sabbatical leave, Workcation

Monetary Benefits

Life insurance from Zurich Austria, Compensation for salary loss during sick leave, 3rd pension pillar contribution, Risk Life Insurance, Meal contribution on top of the legally required minimum, Years of service bonus, Wedding bonus, Baby bonus

Other Benefits

UpDejeneur electronic cafeteria, Public Transport contribution, Maternity leave benefits, Massages in the office

Learning/Development

Professional Certifications, Online Education Portals, Extensive Onboarding program, Strengths based culture (GALLUP)

  • Location(s): Bratislava
  • Hybrid working: It would be nice to meet you in the office 3 times a week, if you live close by
  • Schedule: Full-time

About Us

At our company, we recognize the importance of fostering an open-minded, safe and inclusive environment for everyone. We stand with diversity and respect different backgrounds and lifestyles. That's why we've implemented numerous initiatives to ensure our employees feel comfortable, accepted, and respected at all times.

title: Process Intelligence Specialist
description_html:
Job Description

As a Specialist in the Process Intelligence team within the APEX (AI, Process, Engineering and eXperience) Team, you will interact with stakeholders globally to;

  • Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, document, and analyse business requirements
  • Develop a deep knowledge of core systems to locate appropriate process data
  • Partner with data owners to identify data sources that can be leveraged in process models
  • Produce process models using relevant applications
  • Demonstrate process models and data driven recommendations to end users

Key Responsibilities

Business Process Modelling & Requirements Gathering

  • Assists in modelling current-state and desired business process scenarios, applying corporate modelling standards, definitions, techniques (e.g. BPMN – Business Process Modelling and Notation) and utilizing corporate sanctioned modelling tools (e.g. CPM - Celonis Process Modelling)
  • Conduct thorough analyses of current business processes to identify inefficiencies and opportunities for solutions that leverage AI, Automation or Process Intelligence technologies. Utilize process mapping and modelling techniques to visualize workflows. 
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, document, and analyse business requirements related to automation, AI and process initiatives. Facilitate workshops, interviews, and surveys to elicit functional and non-functional requirements and turn them into testable User Stories.

 Business Process Architecture & Value Analysis

  • Assists in the development and analysis of data driven POV/POC process mining models (simple single object digit twin) to validate assumptions on defined processes
  • Participate in all phases of the development of business process digital twins including defining, documenting, and ensuring traceability of requirements, SDLC documentation, user Acceptance Testing, validation of the identified data sources and data profiling of processes’ digital footprints, sustainment activities to Digital Twins and their KPI Control Towers and associated assets resulting from process updates
  • Utilizing the process intelligence tool set of (1) process models (2) task mining and mapping and (3) digital twins, assists in analysing and documenting efficiencies and improvement opportunities for defined business processes
  • Assists with the identification of process conformance violations & deviations to generate value opportunities and in the development of KPI's based on data points and business opportunity needs

Business Analysis

  • Actively participate in all phases of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), including planning, design, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance. title: Software Engineer
description_html:
Meet the Team

Cisco's Webex Engineering Group is redefining the future of collaboration. We're building a world where people connect effortlessly to enjoy modern, uncompromised collaboration across every room, desk, pocket, and application.

Our technology powers industry-leading products like Slido, Webex Meetings, Webex Calling, and Webex Contact Center. As a member of our team, your work will ensure every user gets a truly immersive experience regardless of device or location, empowering the hybrid working patterns of the future.

Our team is based in Bratislava. We operate a hybrid working model.

Your Impact

  • Develop and maintain software features and improvements to our products.
  • Write clean, secure, and maintainable code following engineering best practices.
  • Debug, test, and fix issues in development and production environments.
  • Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and designers to deliver the right solutions for customers.
  • Learn and apply modern software development tools, frameworks, and workflows and share learnings with the team.
  • Participate in code reviews, incorporate feedback, and continuously improve code quality.
  • Contribute to technical documentation and user-facing documentation as needed.
  • Gain exposure to the operational side of our distributed systems and highly available microservices.
  • Leverage AI technologies to accelerate the team’s productivity and the delivery of customer features.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree (or higher) in a technical field or 1+ year of professional experience in software development.
  • Basic knowledge of software development concepts gained through coursework, projects, or internships and a willingness to learn new technologies quickly.
  • Flexible, thrives in diverse team environments, and communicates clearly and precisely.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Knowledge of the following programming languages: Java or C++ (system development), Node.js, JavaScript, Python is a plus.
  • Knowledge of SQL and NoSQL databases is a plus.
  • Familiar with AI coding tools (Codex, Cursor, Copilot).
  • Familiarity with Git, DevOps, continuous integration, continuous delivery.
  • Strong attention to detail and a commitment to continuous learning and quality results.
  • Ability to apply creative skills to complex problems.

We provide training and mentoring for all new team members to fill in any gaps.

Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

title: Software Compliance Technical Auditor
description_html:
About Us

Always open. Our code, our culture, our opportunities. Leading open innovation without limits. We are SUSE.

SUSE is a global leader in innovative, reliable, and secure enterprise open source solutions, including SUSE® Linux Suite, SUSE® Rancher Suite, SUSE® Edge Suite, and SUSE® AI Suite. More than 60% of the Fortune 500 rely on SUSE to power their mission-critical workloads, enabling them to innovate everywhere – from the data center to the cloud, to the edge and beyond.

SUSE puts the “open” back in open source, collaborating with partners and communities to give customers the agility to tackle innovation challenges today and the freedom to evolve their strategy and solutions tomorrow. For more information, visit www.suse.com.

Software Compliance Technical Auditor

Job Description

The Role

Are you looking to start or grow your career in the world of software licensing and IT data? We are looking for a Software Compliance Technical Auditor. In this role, you will learn how to bridge the gap between technical IT systems and legal software contracts.

This is an entry-point role with a clear and exciting career path at SUSE. You will start by learning the basics of how software is deployed and eventually grow into a specialist or lead auditor.

Career Progression Path

  • You are here: Focus on learning, assisting with audits, and mastering data tools.
  • Next Step: Leading your own audit projects and handling complex technical environments.
  • Future Growth: Designing audit strategies and advising senior leadership.

How You’ll Contribute

As an entry level Auditor, you will work within established guidelines to help our team understand how customers are using SUSE software. You will receive guidance from senior team members as you grow your skills.

  • Learn Technical Discovery: You will help guide customers through the process of running scripts to collect data on their IT systems (Windows, Linux, etc.).
  • Data Analysis: Assist in analyzing "Core" and "Processor" usage in virtual environments like VMware or Cloud platforms (AWS/Azure).
  • Support Reconciliation: Help compare the data we find in a customer’s network against what they are legally allowed to use.
  • Draft Reports: Create initial reports that show findings clearly for both our internal teams and the customer.
  • Solve Problems: Use your logical thinking to figure out why certain data might be missing or how a system is set up.
  • Collaborate: Work closely with your teammates to share what you’ve learned and help improve our audit tools

What You’ll Bring

We are looking for someone who is curious, detail-oriented, and eager to learn.

  • Technical Interest: A basic understanding of Linux (like SLES or Ubuntu) and Windows Server environments.
  • Data Skills: You are proficient with Excel and interested in learning how to use SQL or Python to handle data.
  • Learnability: You love learning new things and can quickly pick up new software tools.
  • Problem-Solving: You enjoy "detective work" and staying focused until you find the answer.
  • Resilience: You can stay positive and professional, even when working on busy projects with deadlines.
  • Communication: You can explain your work clearly to your team and enjoy working with others.
  • Experience: Ideally 1–2 years in IT, data analysis, or a similar field. We value a great attitude and a willingness to grow!

Job

Revenue Generation Non-Sales

What We Offer

We empower you to be bold, driving your career to create the future you want. We celebrate and reward your achievements. 

SUSE is a dynamic environment that is evolving rapidly, thus requiring agility, strong entrepreneurship and an open mind.

This is a compelling opportunity for the right person to join us as we continue to scale and prosper.  

If you’re a big thinker, obsessed by execution and thrive in a dynamic environment in which you can tangibly create a lasting legacy, then please apply now! 

We give you the freedom to be yourself. You will work in a global community of unique individuals – like you – with different backgrounds, talents, skills and perspectives. A truly open community where everyone is welcome, has a voice and is encouraged to reach their full potential regardless of age, gender, race, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, religion, or any other characteristics.  

Sounds like the right fit for you? Click Apply to submit your resume. A recruiter will contact you if your skills match our current or any future positions. In the meantime, stay updated on the latest SUSE news and job vacancies by joining our Talent Community.

SUSE Values

  • Choice
  • Innovation
  • Trust
  • Community

title: Junior / Medior .NET Developer - Application Support
description_html:
FNZ is the power behind many high-scale financial services platforms. Our customers rely on us to create web applications that meet their own high standards for usability and design. These range from highly usable sites for direct investors to sophisticated systems for wealth managers. They always need to be fast, reliable, responsive/adaptive and accessible.

In this exciting role, you will be part of our Production and Support Department, ensuring that all of our live platforms are running smoothly and without issues. This is a great opportunity to kick-start your career and gain commercial experience in programming, growing to become a skilled developer. FNZ offers various career development paths that can be taken, both at our Brno Solution Centre and across the globe.

Responsibilities

  • Investigation of errors in live high-end platforms.
  • Researching and fixing defects based on description.
  • Investigating problems on databases, web and application servers.
  • Developing code, scripts, and schemas required to solve complex technical issues.
  • Dealing with performance issues.
  • Communication with Production Support Analysts and Testers.
  • Automation of manual processes within Production support (CSI).
  • Working in a group of highly-skilled support developers to resolve production issues.

Experience Required

  • University degree in IT or a related field.
  • Good fundamentals in C#, with experience in ASP.NET or VB.NET highly desirable
  • A good understanding of Object-Oriented Programming and the ability to debug (with either commercial experience, or practical knowledge from university projects).
  • Knowledge of MS SQL Database.
  • Willingness to learn new things.
  • A logical thinker who enjoys puzzles and problem-solving.

We Value

  • Knowledge of ITIL.
  • Basics of JavaScript and HTML.
  • Experience with various MS technologies, such as Visual Studio and ReSharper.

Opportunities

  • Be part of a highly successful, rapidly growing, global business – a leader in delivering financial services to some of the world’s largest companies;
  • Remuneration and career advancement is based on individual contribution and business impact, rather than tenure or seniority;
  • We provide significant financial rewards for high-performing individuals; and
  • We provide global career opportunities for our best employees at any of our offices in the UK, EU, US and APAC.

What We Offer

  • 26 days of holiday & 7 sick days per year
  • Home Office support (flexible working hours)
  • Meal Allowance 100 CZK/day
  • Extra paid overtime
  • Multisport card (available also for partner or child)
  • Cafeteria system (600 CZK/month)
  • 26 weeks fully paid maternity and paternity leave (including 26 weeks for the Primary Caregiver and 13 weeks for the Secondary Caregiver)
  • 8 weeks fully paid bereavement leave

  • Pension or Life Insurance (2% from Contractual Monthly Gross Salary)
  • Education and Training (funding of professional exams, LinkedIn trainings)
  • Relocation and Business Trips support
  • Support for statutory leaves (e.g., wedding day, blood donation)
  • Health and Maternity Care
  • Premium and On-call Remuneration
  • Referral program



About FNZ

FNZ is committed to opening up wealth so that everyone, everywhere can invest in their future on their terms. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back.

We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We provide a global, end-to-end wealth management platform that integrates modern technology with business and investment operations. All in a regulated financial institution.

We partner with the world’s leading financial institutions, with over US$2.2 trillion in assets on platform (AoP).

Together with our clients, we empower nearly 30 million people across all wealth segments to invest in their future.

title: QA Engineer
description_html:
For a growing PwC document automation team based in Prague, Czech Republic, we are looking for a QA Engineer who is detail-oriented and passionate about quality assurance.

You will play a key role in validating document automation no code application, identifying issues, and ensuring the reliability of our test strategy. If you have a keen eye for detail, strong analytical skills, and enjoy working in a collaborative environment, we’d be excited to welcome you to our team.

About The Team

Our team has been the driving force for innovation in the area of document automation within the PwC Network. The team operates a document automation platform that is being used in over 50 countries, will soon provide the services to the whole PwC Network globally and expand to external markets. Our team provides a broad range of services, such as solution design, software implementation, software development and integrations, application management and maintenance, robotic process automation or data analytics. A career in our team will provide you with a significant variability of activities, tasks and projects and will enable you to participate on global projects.

About The Manager

Jakub is a Manager in the Document Automation team at PwC, based in Prague. He specializes in optimizing document automation testing, implementing smart automation solutions that enhance efficiency and compliance across client deliverables. His role involves leading projects, coordinating with cross-functional teams, and ensuring high-quality output through innovative use of technology.

Outside of work, Jakub is passionate about motorcycles and enjoys the freedom of riding. He’s also an active participant in various sports, appreciating the team spirit and energy they bring. A lover of culture, Jakub frequently attends concerts, exhibitions, and other cultural events, finding inspiration and balance in the arts.

Job Description & Summary

  • Acquaintance with the specification/list of requirements.
  • Preparation of user high-level test cases and detailed test cases and analysis of labor estimation.
  • Revision of detailed test cases.
  • Synchronization with other testers.
  • Test execution - manual test execution and evaluation.
  • Defect logging.
  • Tracking the status of defects.
  • Preparation of test data.
  • Preparation of documents for the creation of training materials/procedures.

Requirements of the Role

  • Proven experience as a Quality Assurance Engineer/Tester/Test Analyst.
  • Knowledge of Tracking tools (JIRA, Asana, Trello, Azure DevOps)
  • Knowledge of Test Execution tools (Azure DevOps or similar)
  • Proven experience in Software Testing.
  • Fast learning skill.
  • Excellent communication skills.
  • Attention to detail.
  • Czech and English language - both spoken and written

Our Benefits & Fun

  • Concentrated experience and rapid career growth. It may sound like a platitude, but it really is.
  • Paid time off 20 + 5 days, 3 wellbeing days and 2 extra days off from the company at the end of the year.
  • High-end Ultrabook and iPhone with unlimited data.
  • Benefit program with 30.000 points that you can use for holidays, education, food vouchers, sports (Multisport), health... Simply on what you enjoy.
  • Hybrid working model: 3 days office, 2 days (Monday, Friday) home office.
  • Support for your education and development: business and digital skills training (e.g. Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau), ongoing training and certification, for example in Microsoft (Azure, PowerApps), project management (Prince2, PMI), service management (ITIL), agile development (Scrum, SAFe), DAMA, Salesforce, coaching and presentation skills and more. We pay for technical certifications and the time you spend studying them.
  • Opportunity to work in the future at a foreign PwC office, for example in the USA, Canada and there are others in the selection.
  • Extra-work activities and Away Days (team building, ski weekend, etc.).
  • Regular feedback on your work, also consultation with a coach with whom you can address your further development and career direction.

Check us out on: Document Automation | PwC Czech Republic

PwC is the largest audit team in the Czech Republic, but also law, tax, consulting and technology, data or forensic teams. Find out how easy it is to combine this knowledge when you're in the right place. With us, you will get the opportunity to see how business is done in large companies. We are part of an international network of companies with more than 364,000 colleagues in 151 countries. At PwC, we create an inclusive work environment where everyone can be themselves and find their place and opportunity to develop. This year, we have successfully maintained the prestigious Equal Pay Certification, making us one of only four companies in the Czech Republic (and the only one from the Big 4) that demonstrably implements fair compensation practices regardless of gender.

title: Global Bug Reporting Trainee
description_html:
Groupon is a marketplace where customers discover new experiences and services everyday and local businesses thrive. To date we have worked with over a million merchant partners worldwide, connecting over 16 million customers with deals across various categories. In a world often dominated by e-commerce giants, we stand out as one of the few platforms uniquely committed to helping local businesses succeed on a performance basis.

Groupon is on a radical journey to transform our business with relentless pursuit of results. Even with thousands of employees spread across multiple continents, we still maintain a culture that inspires innovation, rewards risk-taking and celebrates success. The impact here can be immediate due to our scale and the speed of our transformation. We're a "best of both worlds" kind of company. We're big enough to have the resources and scale, but small enough that a single person has a surprising amount of autonomy and can make a meaningful impact.

About The Role

Join us as Global Bug Reporting Trainee. Own Groupon's global bug queue for B2B clients. Learn to triage issues, prioritize by impact, and drive fixes across engineering teams in our fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Triage incoming bugs: Analyze logs, APIs (REST/GraphQL), stack traces for root causes.
  • Create minimal repros from vague client reports (steps, payloads, screenshots).
  • Prioritize via impact score: Severity, client count, SLA risks.
  • Manage JIRA backlog: JQL queries, workflows, dashboards; track to deployment.
  • Negotiate with PMs/eng for sprint inclusion; communicate simply to clients.

Key Requirements

  • Interest in L3 support, tech QA, or incident mgmt in SaaS/B2B (1+ years a plus).
  • Basic JIRA and observability tools (Datadog, Kibana, etc.); quick learner.
  • Comfort with logs/APIs/stack traces; repro skills developable.

Nice to Have

  • SW engineering degree or dev/tester cert.
  • Experience with financial/SLA impact scoring.

What Success Looks Like

Bugs triaged accurately; contribute to faster fixes; grow into independent queue owner.

We Are Interested In

Trainees eager to thrive in chaos, take ownership, and use data/AI for triage.

Groupon is an AI-First Company

We’re committed to building smarter, faster, and more innovative ways of working—and AI plays a key role in how we get there. We encourage candidates to leverage AI tools during the hiring process where it adds value, and we’re always keen to hear how technology improves the way you work. If you’re passionate about AI or curious to explore how it can elevate your role—you’ll be right at home here.

Groupon’s purpose is to build strong communities through thriving small businesses. To learn more about the world’s largest local e-commerce marketplace, click here. You can also find out more about us in the latest Groupon news as well as learning about our DEI approach. If all of this sounds like something that’s a great fit for you, then click apply and join us on a mission to become the ultimate destination for local experiences and services.

Beware of Recruitment Fraud: Groupon follows a merit-based recruitment process without charging job seekers any fees.
